title: podman adds host.containers.internal to /etc/hosts while running rootless
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The addition of the `hosts.container.internal` entry to /etc/hosts seems to indicate that you can connect back to the host from a rootless container. But the IP that is given is the gateway in the rootless netns `podman unshare --rootless-netns` so that cannot work (according to @Luap99 ).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. rootless podman
2. `podman network create some-random-network`
3. `podman run --network some-random-network -it --rm alpine grep "host.containers.internal" /etc/hosts`

**Describe the results you received:**

`10.89.4.1 host.containers.internal`

**Describe the results you expected:**

Expected result is no `hosts.container.internal` entry.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.3
API Version:  4.0.3
Go Version:   go1.18.1
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
This is already fixed in main, should be in v4.1.
https://github.com/containers/common/blob/4081e6be935620957277ad76df699dfa0b3a1ffc/libnetwork/etchosts/ip.go#L29-L33
